title.es: Loss Functions, Volatility Drag y Expected Loss
title.en: Loss Functions, Volatility Drag and Expected Loss

summary_es: Este modulo construye el framework de decisiones probabilisticas desde loss functions hasta preservacion de capital. Se definen loss matrices para decisiones de inversion (invertir, no invertir, cubrir) y se calcula expected loss integrando la distribucion posterior predictiva. Se comparan VaR y Expected Shortfall mostrando que ES captura riesgo de cola que VaR ignora. Se demuestra volatility drag: la varianza destruye riqueza incluso cuando los retornos promedian cero, con drag proporcional a sigma^2/2. Se analiza path dependence mostrando que el orden de retornos importa cuando hay flujos intermedios. Se conecta con la preservacion de capital como prioridad fundamental.
summary_en: This module builds the probabilistic decision framework from loss functions to capital preservation. Loss matrices are defined for investment decisions (invest, hold, hedge) and expected loss is calculated integrating the posterior predictive distribution. VaR and Expected Shortfall are compared, showing ES captures tail risk that VaR ignores. Volatility drag is demonstrated: variance destroys wealth even when returns average zero, with drag proportional to sigma^2/2. Path dependence shows return order matters with intermediate cash flows. Connected to capital preservation as fundamental priority.
learning_objectives_es: Definir loss matrices y calcular expected loss con distribuciones predictivas
Comparar VaR vs Expected Shortfall y entender por que ES es superior
Demostrar volatility drag: la varianza destruye riqueza (drag ~ sigma^2/2)
Analizar path dependence y su impacto en decisiones con flujos intermedios
Integrar loss functions con posterior predictive para decisiones optimas
learning_objectives_en: Define loss matrices and calculate expected loss with predictive distributions
Compare VaR vs Expected Shortfall and understand why ES is superior
Demonstrate volatility drag: variance destroys wealth (drag ~ sigma^2/2)
Analyze path dependence and its impact on decisions with intermediate flows
Integrate loss functions with posterior predictive for optimal decisions
